differential_diagnoses: Metastatic carcinoma (e.g., lung, renal, or hepatocellular)
Right atrial thrombus (secondary to pacemaker lead)
next_steps: Image-guided tissue biopsy of the right atrial mass
Positron emission tomography-computed tomography (PET-CT) to evaluate for primary malignancy or systemic metastatic disease
Cardiac magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) for tissue characterization and to evaluate for myocardial infiltration
Multidisciplinary review involving cardiology, cardiothoracic surgery, and oncology
Maintain anticoagulation and monitor for signs of cardiac tamponade or worsening right heart failure

differential_diagnoses: Right atrial thrombus (secondary to device/lead infection or hypercoagulability)
next_steps: Verify MRI-compatibility of the current pacemaker system to facilitate Cardiac MRI
Cardiac MRI with gadolinium contrast for tissue characterization and to differentiate tumor from thrombus
PET-CT to identify potential primary malignancy and systemic metastatic burden
Thoracentesis with cytological and immunohistochemical analysis of the pleural effusion
Multidisciplinary tumor board consultation (Cardiothoracic surgery, Oncology, Cardiology, Pathology)
Ultrasound-guided or image-guided biopsy of the atrial mass or lymph nodes if surgical exploration is not immediately feasible
Continued anticoagulation for pulmonary embolism while monitoring for hemorrhagic risk
